channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Ring’s Super Bowl Ad Prompted a Public Freakout. Wyze Turned It Into a Marketing Win
item.description: One brand’s advertising loss is another brand’s advertising victory.

item.title: CEOs are finally speaking out about ICE. Is corporate activism back?
item.description: For weeks, corporate leaders across the country largely stayed silent as immigration officers descended on the city of Minneapolis, eventually killing...

item.title: The tech billionaires aren’t just grabbing trophy Florida mansions—they have competing half-billion-dollar megayachts jostling for dock space
item.description: $500,000 annual docking fees and private marinas are all part of the package for Florida’s wealthy seagoers.

item.title: These popular air purifiers are being recalled over fire risk
item.description: Airova is recalling 191,390 Aroeve air purifiers over concerns they could “overheat and ignite, posing fire and burn hazards to consumers,” according ...

item.title: This New Ad Agency Has a Secret Advantage, and It’s Not What You Think
item.description: The Ability Machine has already completed brand work for Mercedes and Kind.

item.title: Hackers Are Hammering Google’s Gemini With Prompts to Steal the LLM—Every AI Company Should Be Worried
item.description: Google says smaller companies are next.

item.title: Chipotle Has a 4-Part Plan to Boost Flat Sales. Part 1 Is ‘the Most Celebrated Limited-Time Offer in History’
item.description: In an earnings call, CEO Scott Boatwright said the Mexican restaurant chain will increase its ‘menu innovation cadence’ in 2026.

item.title: Estée Lauder tested products sold on Walmart’s site. What it found led to a lawsuit
item.description: Estée Lauder has accused Walmart of selling counterfeit beauty goods on its website in a lawsuit filed in California federal court earlier this week t...

item.title: Instagram boss reveals he’s paid $900k per year plus stock worth ‘tens of millions of dollars’ as he denies ‘addiction’ claims
item.description: The plaintiff’s lawyer in a landmark social media addiction trial tried to connect Adam Mosseri’s compensation with Instagram’s policy toward filters.

item.title: The affordability crisis is driving unprecedented price cuts in the housing market, Realtor.com says
item.description: “Builders are clearly responding to today’s affordability pressures,” said Realtor.com chief economist Danielle Hale.

item.title: These digital tools are stepping up the global fight against wildlife trafficking
item.description: In late 2025, Interpol coordinated a global operation across 134 nations, seizing roughly 30,000 live animals , confiscating illegal plant and timber ...

item.title: Homes sales fell 8.4% in January despite lower mortgage rates
item.description: Sales of previously occupied U.S. homes fell sharply in January as higher home prices and possibly harsh winter weather kept many prospective homebuye...

item.title: OpenClaw is the bad boy of AI agents. Here’s why security experts say you should beware
item.description: The open-source AI agent pushes autonomy to the edge — thrilling hobbyists and unnerving security experts

item.title: One of Wall Street’s most feared hedge fund managers on the decline of the dollar: gold is ‘becoming the reserve asset’
item.description: Greenlight Capital's David Einhorn, known for his “Einhorn Effect” and years of touting gold, says the world's central banks don't love dollars anymor...

item.title: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Are Joining Forces on an AI Startup Accelerator
item.description: Meta, Microsoft, and Sequoia are also participating in the F/ai startup accelerator program in Paris.
